name: Nigeria

colors.symbolism: Nigeria's natural wealth and agriculture; the lush vegetation of the country

description: Three equal vertical stripes — green at the hoist, white in the centre, green at the fly. Aspect ratio 1:2.
history: Adopted on 1 October 1960, the day Nigeria gained independence from the United Kingdom. The design was selected from approximately 2,870 entries in a 1959 nationwide competition; the winning entry was submitted by Michael Taiwo Akinkunmi, then a 23-year-old student in London. His original design also included a red sun emblem on the white stripe; the judges removed the sun, leaving the simple green-white-green tricolour.
